text:
Left Brennmoor before first light, before anyone could ask where I was headed or why. The garrison gates were still frost-locked; the watchman on duty owed me a favor from the flood two winters back, and didn't ask questions.

The road south is quieter than I remember. No caravans this time of year, just the wind moving through the dead reed fields. I keep thinking I hear hoofbeats behind me. There aren't any.
